latest.title: Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd: Director Dealing
latest.content:
Today 07:00
Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd | Company announcement | March 19, 2026 at 09:00:00 EET
TURKU, FINLAND â€" Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel immunotherapies, announces today that Pacta sunt servanda Oy, an entity wholly owned by Vesa Karvonen, General Counsel of the Company, subscribed 72,000 ordinary shares in Faron at price of EUR 0.5 per share on 18 March 2026, pursuant to the rights issue announced by the Company on 10 March 2026.
Further details are set out in the Notification of Dealing Form below.
For more information, please contact:
IR Partners, Finland(Media)Kare Laukkanen
+358 50 553 9535 / +44 7 469 766 223
kare.laukkanen@irpartners.fi
FINN Partners, US(Media) Alyssa Paldo
+1 847 791-8085
alyssa.paldo@finnpartners.com
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP(Nominated Adviser and Broker)Sandy Jamieson, Jo Turner
+44 (0) 207 213 0880
Sisu Partners Oy(Certified Adviser on Nasdaq First North)Juha KarttunenJukka J¤rvel¤
+358 (0)40 555 4727+358 (0)50 553 8990
About bexmarilimabBexmarilimab is Faronâ€™s wholly owned, investigational immunotherapy designed to overcome resistance to existing treatments and optimize clinical outcomes, by targeting myeloid cell function and igniting the immune system. Bexmarilimab binds to Clever-1, an immunosuppressive receptor found on macrophages leading to tumor growth and metastases (i.e. helps cancer evade the immune system). By targeting the Clever-1 receptor on macrophages, bexmarilimab alters the tumor microenvironment, reprogramming macrophages from an immunosuppressive (M2) state to an immunostimulatory (M1) one, upregulating interferon production and priming the immune system to attack tumors and sensitizing cancer cells to standard of care.
About Faron Pharmaceuticals LtdFaron (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON) is a global, clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focused on tackling cancers via novel immunotherapies. Its mission is to bring the promise of immunotherapy to a broader population by uncovering novel ways to control and harness the power of the immune system. The Companyâ€™s lead asset is bexmarilimab, a novel anti-Clever-1 humanized antibody, with the potential to remove immunosuppression of cancers through reprogramming myeloid cell function. Bexmarilimab is being investigated in Phase I/II clinical trials as a potential therapy for patients with hematological cancers in combination with other standard treatments. Further information is available at
www.faron.com

company_30s.what_they_do: Faron Pharmaceuticals develops and markets small‑molecule treatments for rare autoimmune diseases, with a focus on a lead candidate that blocks a key inflammatory pathway.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company is tackling a niche yet sizable market—patients with hard‑to‑treat conditions that currently have limited options. If its lead drug reaches regulatory approval, it could capture a sizable share of a multi‑billion‑pound therapeutic area.
company_30s.current_state: Faron has been burning cash to fund late‑stage trials and a rights offering. The business is still in a pre‑commercial stage, with revenues pending clinical milestones. The shares are trading at a steep discount to the price level seen before the rights issue.

rns_narrative: The company has announced a fully covered rights offering that will raise roughly €40 million to shore up its balance sheet and fund the next phase of its lead drug. The announcement follows a series of updates that confirm the timing of the annual general meeting and the approval of the offering by the Finnish regulator.

These filings show a company that is actively managing its capital structure, moving from a high‑cash‑burn phase to a more sustainable footing. The rights issue is a sign that management is trying to keep the pipeline alive while giving existing shareholders a chance to buy in at a lower price.
The narrative therefore shifts from a survival story to a stabilization story—Faron is taking concrete steps to secure the capital needed for its next clinical milestone.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly anxious – the community is listening for the next rights‑issue result.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter revolves around the potential of the rights issue to give shareholders a cheaper entry point and the belief that a successful trial could lift the stock sharply.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices worry that the company’s cash burn remains unsustainable and that the rights offering may dilute shareholders further without delivering immediate value.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past week, sentiment has been flat but slightly leaning toward caution; no new catalysts have emerged, keeping the mood subdued.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is essentially dead, meaning there is almost no public awareness—great for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the rights offering and a clear timetable for the next phase of clinical trials.
Any regulatory feedback or an advisory committee meeting that could signal a positive or negative opinion on the lead product.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 50‑day average, indicating that institutional money is moving in.
A press release confirming a milestone such as a positive Phase II interim analysis.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the share price for a breakout above the recent 20‑day moving average while volume stays above the 50‑day average. Also keep an eye on the timing of the AGM—if the company announces a new funding round or a partnership, that would strengthen the bullish case.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery: the recent drop has been sharp but the price has already bottomed out near the 52‑week low. The APEX score suggests a tight compression that could trigger a bounce if the right catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep discount is a classic contrarian signal. If the rights issue closes at a favorable price and the company meets its next clinical milestone, the stock could rebound to the midpoint of its recent trading range—offering a potential upside that far exceeds the limited downside because the price is already near its lowest point.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the company may fail to secure additional funding or that the clinical program stalls, which would keep the share price stuck at the low. A realistic downside is a further decline to a price that reflects the company’s current cash‑burn trajectory, but that is still well above the absolute bottom.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose up to the current price if the program fails, but a recovery to the mid‑range could more than double the investment. The asymmetry is clear: small loss, big upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at a break of the 20‑day moving average with a stop just below the recent 20‑day low. Size the position to allow for a 30‑day holding period, taking advantage of the expected mean‑reversion window.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the rights offering to close and for any announcement that the company has secured a new funding source or a positive trial result. A volume spike of at least 1.5× the 50‑day average would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The company has not shown any signs of imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Faron’s steep discount, a fresh capital raise, and a quiet market create a contrarian setup with high upside if the next clinical milestone is met, while the downside is limited to the current low.
